_doc: R5-018 ground-truth corpus for the architect's decision-mode classifier (collaborator vs replacement). Each entry: description (task summary), expected_mode, rationale. Heuristic-as-proxy: keyword sets approximate the architect prompt's STEP 1.6 logic.

entries.description: Add a new feature page that lets users save favorite items to their profile.
entries.rationale: User-facing feature, business rule, UX decision.

entries.description: Refactor the database migration to add a partial index on orders.status.
Decide on the pricing tier copy and CTA wording for the upgrade modal.
Tune the connection pool size and JVM heap for the staging cluster.
Design the onboarding flow for first-time users.
Implement OAuth2 PKCE flow with refresh-token rotation.
Choose icons and colors for the status badges in the admin dashboard.
Set up a CI pipeline that runs unit tests, lint, and type-check on every PR.
Write a business rule that calculates discounts for loyalty-program members.
Add a Redis cache layer in front of the products API with a 5-minute TTL.
Decide which fields should be visible on the public profile vs private.
entries.rationale: User-facing privacy, business rule — product domain.

entries.description: Migrate from REST polling to WebSocket push for live updates.
Author the marketing landing page hero section copy.
Configure Postgres replication with streaming and a hot-standby replica.
Define the empty-state message and illustration for an empty cart.
Add structured logging with trace IDs across all microservices.
Choose the default sort order for search results based on user research.
Implement constant-time string comparison for password hash verification.
Plan a feature that lets sellers respond to buyer questions in-app.
Switch the queue backend from in-memory to RabbitMQ with dead-letter handling.
Decide whether to require email verification before account activation.
entries.rationale: User-facing flow, business rule — product domain.

entries.description: Optimize the N+1 query in the dashboard endpoint with a join.
